evals.name: vietnamese-dental-implant-competitors
evals.prompt: Analyze these competitor dental implant landing pages: https://example-nhakhoa-a.vn/implant, https://example-nhakhoa-b.vn/trong-rang-implant, https://example-nhakhoa-c.vn/cay-ghep-implant. The project is 'dental-implant-clinic'.
expected_behavior: Confirms the 3 URLs with user before crawling
Attempts web_fetch on each URL; handles failures gracefully
If web_fetch fails, offers copy-paste HTML fallback with clear instructions
Scores each competitor across all 6 dimensions (MESSAGE, STRUCTURE, TRUST, CTA, DESIGN, TECH) with 1-10 scores
Extracts exact h1 text, CTA button text, form field count from each page
Detects framework (WordPress, React, etc.) and analytics (GTM, GA)
Identifies common patterns (table stakes), differentiators, and gaps
Creates swipe file with best headline, CTA, trust approach from across competitors
Produces competitor scoreboard comparison table
Shows progress indicators: [competitor-analysis] Step N/6
Suggests next step: run audience-persona

evals.name: keyword-based-saas-competitors
evals.prompt: Find and analyze competitors for our project management SaaS. Use keywords: 'best project management tool', 'Monday.com alternative'. Project name is 'project-management-saas'. I don't have specific URLs.
expected_behavior: Uses web_search with provided keywords to find competitor pages
Extracts 3-5 competitor URLs from search results (sponsored + organic)
Presents found URLs to user for confirmation before crawling
If niche-brief.md exists for this project, also uses its top keywords
Handles mix of fetchable and unfetchable pages gracefully
If a page is SPA/JavaScript-rendered, detects empty body and asks for rendered HTML paste
Scores all successfully fetched competitors on 6 dimensions
Cross-competitor comparison identifies SaaS-specific patterns (free trial CTAs, feature comparison tables, integration logos)
Swipe file highlights best SaaS landing page elements
Report notes any competitors that could not be analyzed with reason
Saves both competitor-report.md and swipe-file.md to correct output path

evals.name: edge-case-broken-urls
evals.prompt: Analyze these competitors for my-bakery project: https://thisdomaindoesnotexist12345.com/landing, https://httpstat.us/500, https://httpstat.us/403. All three URLs are the ones I have.
expected_behavior: Confirms the 3 URLs with user before attempting to crawl
Attempts web_fetch on each URL sequentially
First URL: DNS failure or connection error — logs failure, tries www. variant, then offers paste fallback
Second URL: HTTP 500 — logs 'server error', offers alternate URL or paste fallback
Third URL: HTTP 403 — logs 'access denied', tries http:// variant, then offers paste fallback
Does NOT crash or abandon the entire skill after first failure
After all 3 fail, tells user: 'Could not fetch any competitor pages. To proceed, please paste HTML source or provide different URLs.'
Does NOT produce a competitor-report.md with zero analyzed competitors
If user pastes HTML for at least 1 page, continues with partial analysis and notes which competitors were skipped
If user says 'skip all', gracefully exits and suggests running with different URLs
